sourcePath: documents/Sent By Anjan/program_content/men sexual health 30 days.md

cards.goal: Start your recovery by reducing stimulation, activating your body, and building awareness.

cards.title: Today's Focus
paragraphs: Start your recovery by reducing stimulation, activating your body, and building awareness.
cards.highlight: Progress comes from consistency, not intensity alone.

cards.title: Reset + Morning Start
instructions: When the urge appears, use CALM, delay 5–10 minutes, and shift focus.
Drink 1–2 glasses of water after waking.
Optional: add soaked almonds or raisins.
cards.whyThisWorks: Reducing stimulation and giving the body an early reset builds control and steadier recovery momentum.

cards.title: Breathing Exercise (2 minutes)
cards.instructions: • Sit comfortably and relax your body • Inhale through your nose → 4 seconds • Exhale slowly → 6 seconds Repeat for 2 minutes • reduces stress and anxiety • improves focus and control • calms the nervous system

exercises.name: Pelvic Strength — Hold & Release
instructions: 16 reps × 1 sets Rest 15 seconds between sets

exercises.name: Pelvic Activation — Pillow Squeeze Kegels
instructions: • Lie on your back with knees bent
• Place a pillow between your thighs
12–15 reps × 1 sets Hold 5–7 seconds Rest 15 seconds between sets

exercises.name: Hip & Glute Strength — Glute Bridge March
instructions: • Lie on your back with knees bent
• Lift hips into a glute bridge position
• While holding the bridge, lift one foot slightly
22 alternating marches × 1 sets Rest 20 seconds between sets

cards.title: Night Routine
instructions: Do slow breathing and relax your body.
cards.whyThisWorks: improves blood circulation reduces pressure in pelvic area supports natural recovery

cards.prompt: What helped you most on Day 1?
cards.helperText: Write one or two practical observations.

cards.secondaryMessage: Start your recovery by reducing stimulation, activating your body, and building awareness.

cards.instructions: • Sit comfortably and relax your body • Inhale → 4 seconds • Exhale → 6 seconds • On each exhale, feel your body relax Repeat for 3 minutes • reduces stress • improves control over urges • builds mind-body awareness

exercises.name: Pelvic Activation — Pillow Squeeze Kegels
instructions: 15 reps × 1 sets Hold 6–7 seconds Rest 15 seconds

instructions: Do slow breathing and relax your body.
